page: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel architecture running at a base frequency of 3.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 60.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included with the instance.

Performance benchmarks position the c2-60 in the average tier across most standardized tests. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks yield scores of 1535.88 and 24461.89 respectively, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 9363.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1775.09 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 45.09 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves a static web serving throughput of 16.25 GB/sec and processes 25.27 million Redis SET operations per second.

The hardware configuration presents clear tradeoffs for modern workloads. While the dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading offer predictable compute performance, the older Intel Haswell architecture lacks modern instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. This limitation is reflected in weak LLM text generation speeds, which drop to 6.54 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model. Consequently, this instance is best suited for traditional compute-bound tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations rather than modern machine learning inference.
description: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel architecture running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 60.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance delivers stable, dedicated CPU performance with a 1:1 physical-to-virtual core ratio. Standardized benchmarks place its CPU and memory performance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9363.0 and a static web serving throughput of 16.25 GB/sec. Due to the lack of modern AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets, the server exhibits weak performance in LLM text generation. It is highly suitable for traditional compute-bound workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized KVM instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel cores, 60 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, delivering reliable performance for compilation and web serving.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-60 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel cores, 60 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD for traditional compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core compute-optimized instance with local SSD storage, designed for predictable processing workloads and software compilation.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 3.0 GHz without hyperthreading
60.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB per core
Bundled 400 GB local SSD storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance suitable for software compilation and database operations
Weak performance in modern machine learning and LLM text generation tasks
